calendar.matters:
Second Amended Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition Of Kelly Withem To Perpetuate Testimony Of Robert Withem By Videotaped Deposition
Off Calendar Sep-03-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing through CourtCall for petitioner and moving party Kelly Withem.
Ryan T. Moore of BERKES CRANE ROBINSON & SEAL LLP (213-955-1150), specially appearing through CourtCall for Defendant Plant Products & Supply Co.
Charles S. Bishop of SINUNU BRUNI, LLP (415-362-9700), specially appearing through CourtCall for defendant Fryer-Knowles of California.
Robert Baronian of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), specially appearing through CourtCall for defendant Chevron Entities.
Jon C. James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), specially appearing through CourtCall for defendant Fryer-Knowles, Inc. a Washington Corporation.
Hanna M. Thompson of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), specially appearing through CourtCall for defendant Riley Power Inc.
Patrick M. Mahoney of BHC Law Group, LLP (510-658-3600), specially appearing through CourtCall for defendant M. Slayen & Associates, Inc.
Christine Hawkins of HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG (415-766-3200) specially appearing through CourtCall for defendant Marconi Plastering Inc.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: The Petition of Kelly Withem to Perpetuate Testimony of Robert Withem by Videotaped deposition is GRANTED pursuant to C.C.P. 2035.010. Petitioner filed proofs of service as to all prospective defendants named in the petition. Opposition filed by Fryer-Knowles, Inc. a Washington Corporation, and joinder by Plant Products & Supply Co. The Court denies the conditions requested by Fryer-Knowles Inc. on pages 5-7 of its opposition, except for the condition that the parties meet and confer as to a mutually convenient date, time, and place for the deposition. Order to include language: all parties are to file a notice of special appearance after 48 hours of receipt of served order. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312 and consistent with the tentative ruling. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
